##NAMES=Jahnsite-(MnMnZn)
##RRUFFID=R250071
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Mn^2+^Mn^2+^Zn_2_Fe^3+^_2_(PO_4_)_4_(OH)_2_·8H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Herdade dos Pendões mine, Beja, Portugal
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Sub-parallel bundles of light golden brown prisms  or thick blades formed in a highly altered gossan in association with Zn-bearing libethenite, quartz, rhodochrosite and santabarbaraite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2018)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250071
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Mn^2+^_0.71_Na_0.15_Ca_0.10_)(Mn^2+^_1.00_)(Zn_1.00_Mn^2+^_0.66_Fe^2+^_0.21_Mg_0.10_Fe^3+^_0.03_)(Fe^3+^_1.99_Al_0.01_)(P_1.01_O_4_)_4_(OH)_2_·8H_2_0
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 15.222 b: 7.187 c: 10.028 alpha: 90 beta: 111.746 gamma: 90 volume: 1019 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
